COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Additional Deposition Testimony Of W.J. Broadbent Based On Improper Instructions Not To Answer And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-02-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
Mark C. Goodman, Esq., appeared for defendant and moving party: Boards More, GMBH
Matt Davis, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff and opposing party: William Joel Broadbent

RULING:
HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Boards And More GmbH's Motion To Compel Additional Testimony Of Plaintiff Joel Broadbent Based Upon Improper Instructions Not To Answer is denied. The questions about medications in 2004 and recreational drugs at any time before December 2004 are overly broad and invade Plaintiff's right to privacy without good cause. The questions about the identity of lawyers Plaintiff may have talked with regarding the retention of Mr. Davis are irrelevant.
The court finds that Defendant failed adequately to meet and confer, and further finds that the motion was brought without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $1,000.00 to be paid within 30 days of notice of this motion. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
